title: Xtra Smoky Algerian lamb Tagine
description: A charred and pillowy solo dinner dish featuring minced garlic, this Algerian tagine is low in calories and rich in flavor. It is made with tender lamb and a blend of aromatic spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the smoked paprika, ground cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the diced tomatoes and chicken broth, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the tagine for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, and skim off any excess fat.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, and accompanied by a side of couscous or crusty bread.
